course_description:
Intermediate study of Western classical music ensemble preparation and performance

Intermediate study of Western classical music ensemble preparation and performance

Students taking this course participate in and are assessed on their workﾠin one of five different ensemble options:

Each option has a different application process, and students are encouraged to provisionally enrol in the course while going through the application process. If you are not successful in your application, you must either apply for another option or drop your enrolment in the course.

UQ Chorale, Symphony Orchestra, Wind Symphony

These options are open to the wider UQ community, and allow UQ students who are not enrolled in this course to participate as an extension activity or for personal enjoyment. Please follow the process atﾠ https://music.uq.edu.au/current-students/auditioning-uq-ensembles to audition for these ensembles.

Chamber Music

This option is only open to Bachelor of Music students. To apply, your chamber group must submit an expression of interest to Dr Anna Grinberg by 12pm on theﾠFriday of Orientation Week. Late applications will be considered on a case by case basis up to 12pm on the Friday of Week Two.

Your chamber group must have a minimum of three members (two for groups that only include pianists and percussionists). Your chamber group may include one BMus student who is not enrolled in this option in an Ensemble Studies course and who isﾠparticipating in chamber music as an extension activity. In 2024, your chamberﾠgroup may also include an additional BMus student who is not enrolled if they are playing an upper strings or lower strings instrument in your chamber group.

Your expression of interest must include the following:

If your expression of interest is accepted, you will be advised of your assigned chamber music tutor(s) and of your approved repertoire by the course coordinator.

This option is only open toﾠBachelor of Music students who are studying voice in Performance Studies. To apply, please email Dr Shaun Brown ﾠby 12pm on theﾠFriday of Orientation Week.

This course has been developed with consideration of Indigenising the curriculum.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Students in this course are expected to attend all classes, rehearsals and performances for the ensemble on which they successfully auditioned. Any requests for planned absences must be provided in writing to the relevant teacher(s) and course coordinator well in advance, and medical certificates for unplanned absences must be provided as soon as possible following the period of absence.
course_aims: This intermediate course develops your capacity and ability to rehearse and perform as part of an ensemble.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate your understanding of effective collaborative approaches to the preparation of ensemble repertoire for performance at an intermediate level.
Identify and overcome challenges in the preparation of individual parts in a manner that supports being an effective ensemble performer at intermediate level
Demonstrate your awareness of professional expectations surrounding ensemble performance in varied contexts at an intermediate level

assessment_details.title: Hurdle Task - Week 2 - Chamber Music ONLY
assessment_details.task_description: Chamber Music Ensembles will consult with their assigned tutor or Course Coordinator in order to confirm the suitability of their project and develop a framework for the semester's work. Repertoire will also be confirmed for the hurdle requirement with the designated tutors. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Email your submission to a.grinberg@uq.edu.au.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

assessment_details.title: Performance Report/Director's Report
assessment_details.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration
assessment_details.due_date: Week 1 Mon - Week 6 Fri Precise times of assessment activities will be communicated via Blackboard. Due dates will vary for each ensemble.
assessment_details.task_description: Students will receive an Interim assessment comprising 40% of the assessment for this course. Rubrics or marking criteria will be made available on Blackboard before the start of semester. For UQ Chorale and Stagecraft, this is provided by the ensemble leader no later than the end of Week 6. For UQ Symphony Orchestra, this assessment will be practical sectionals. A report form will be provided to the student by the end of Week 6. For UQ Wind Symphony , this assessment will be practical sectionals. A report form will be provided to the student by the end of Week 6. For Chamber Music , students must perform between 10-15 minutes of selected repertoire in week 6 in an open masterclass setting and receive a written report the following week. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Not a submissible item, assessment is performance based throughout the first half of semester.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
An extension/deferral is not possible on assessment based on participation and engagement with group learning activities undertaken throughout the semester.

assessment_details.task_description: At the completion of the activities for the semester a final report for UQSO , UQ Chorale, UQWS members and those studying Stagecraft is provided by the Ensemble Director or UQ assessor. At the completion of the semester a final performance report for Chamber Music is provided in response to a 15-20 min performance of selected repertoire. All students in the Chamber Music stream must attend this performance forum. Any rubrics or marking criteria will be made available on Blackboard before the start of semester. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. learning_resources.other_course_materials:
If we've listed something under further requirement, you'll need to provide your own. Required Item Description Further Requirement Instruments Students are expected to bring their own instruments to all ensemble activities as advised. Students who are either required or invited to participate in any ensemble activity are expected to bring their own instrument to rehearsals and performances, with the following exceptions where the School will provide the instruments:

cor anglais
bass clarinet
contra-bassoon
percussion own item needed Scores A range of public domain and copyrighted materials will be required for the various elements of this course. Music scores for choral works may have to be purchased, hired or downloaded. Orchestral scores will be provided. Chamber Music scores can be hired from UQ library or accessed through public domain. Please see Blackboard for details. own item needed Hearing Protection Students and staff participating in some practical activities are exposed to hazardous noise, and must have appropriate hearing protection to reduce the level of exposure so it is safe to participate in those activities. Students must obtain and use approved hearing protection to participate in these classes.

Current approved options are:
- custom fitted musician moulds with at least 10dB attenuation purchased at the UQ Health and Rehabilitation Clinics through the Audiology Clinic.
- Etymotic ER20 ETY High Fidelity Ear Plugs

Other hearing protection must be reviewed by the Health, Safety and Wellness Coordinator before being approved for use.

Additional activities may be identified during the semester as a result of our ongoing testing processes, and announced on Blackboard. own item needed

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Chamber Ensemble Tutorials All student-led chamber music ensembles will receive 6 hours of tutorials from relevant performance staff, distributed regularly throughout the semester (with a minimum of 3 to be scheduled before the mid semester break). Dates and times for tutorials will be scheduled in consultation with the allocated ensemble tutor(s). Tutors expect demonstrable progress between tutorials and for student ensemble leaders to maintain regular contact. Ensembles must rehearse regularly (multiple times per week) and group members should divide responsibility equitably for different undertakings to ensure the smooth functioning of the ensemble.(For example - librarian, room bookings, rehearsal set-up and tutorial organisation.) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Stagecraft This component of the Ensemble Studies course is open only to vocal students with interest in developing their skills in the field of opera. In this part of the Ensembles Studies course, particular emphasis will be placed on a singer’s presentation, movement, and communication with an audience. This will enable participants in this course to do more than just sing; as opera combines music and theatre, opera singers combine their vocal and musical skill with convincing acting and movement ability in order to bring the work of librettists and composers to life. All students in this activity are expected to attend regular timetabled weekly classes, any additional classes and activities that are scheduled in the lead-up to performances, and those performances. Dates and times for additional rehearsals and performances will be announced on Blackboard as the semester progresses. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: UQ Chorale Rehearsals and Performances All students in this ensemble are expected to attend regular timetabled weekly rehearsals, any additional rehearsals that are scheduled in the lead-up to performances, and those performances. Dates and times for additional rehearsals and performances will be announced on Blackboard as the semester progresses. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: Private Practice All streams of activity in this course require diligent individual practice and preparation (including non-teaching weeks). You may also wish to use some time in your lessons in Performance Studies to cover some repertoire in Ensemble Studies. This will be at the discretion of your principal tutor. Learning outcomes: L02, L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: UQ Wind Symphony Rehearsals and Performances All students who successfully auditioned for this ensemble are expected to practice and prepare parts to a high level for rehearsals and performances. Students are expected to attend all rehearsals. In addition to the scheduled rehearsal time, Wind Symphony members are expected to assist with the setup and bump-out procedures each week as this builds industry practice knowledge in wind symphony functioning within the industry. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

UQ Symphony Orchestra Rehearsals and Performances All students who successfully auditioned for this ensemble are expected to practice and prepare parts to a high level leading up to the start of intensive activity focused in and around Project Week. Due to the intensive nature of study in this stream (there are no timetabled weekly rehearsals), students must commit to attend all rehearsals without exception. Most rehearsals will take place after hours between 6.30-9.30pm. All of the evenings of Project Week should be reserved for this activity in addition to the weekend of the performance. The final timetable for rehearsals will be finalised by the end of Week 4 of semester. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
